title: Englerophoenix caribaeum
taxonomy: Englerophoenix caribaeum is a species of palm tree in the family Arecaceae. It is native to the Caribbean and is closely related to other palm species in the same family. The classification of Englerophoenix caribaeum places it within the subfamily Arecoideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies of palms. Englerophoenix caribaeum is further divided into several infraspecific taxa, reflecting its regional variability. The classification of Englerophoenix caribaeum continues to be refined through ongoing research in palm systematics.

intro: Englerophoenix caribaeum, also known as the Caribbean or West Indian fan palm, is a species of palm tree native to the Caribbean and the northern coast of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Englerophoenix caribaeum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever and infection.
